step_1_context.title: Context & Discovery
step_1_context.project_vision: Transform Nexus Financial Services' customer engagement model by implementing an AI-powered Customer Intelligence Platform that delivers personalized financial insights, predictive analytics, and automated advisory services to 2.5M+ retail banking customers. This platform will integrate with existing core banking systems while establishing a modern, scalable data architecture that reduces customer churn by 25% and increases cross-sell revenue by 40% within 18 months of deployment.
step_1_context.business_context: Nexus Financial Services operates in a highly competitive fintech landscape with legacy infrastructure that limits personalization capabilities. Current customer data is siloed across 12+ systems, preventing a unified customer view. Regulatory pressures (GDPR, CCPA, SOX) require enhanced data governance. The board has approved a $4.2M digital transformation initiative with Q4 2025 as the target launch date.

business_objectives.specific: Boost average revenue per customer by 35%
business_objectives.achievable: Through AI-driven product recommendations and proactive engagement
business_objectives.relevant: Directly impacts revenue growth strategic pillar

business_objectives.specific: Decrease annual churn rate from 18% to 12%
business_objectives.relevant: Customer retention is top executive priority

business_objectives.specific: Automate 60% of tier-1 customer support queries
business_objectives.measurable: Ticket deflection rate and CSAT scores

stakeholders.name: Dr. Emily Watson
stakeholders.title: Chief Data Officer
stakeholders.responsibility: Data architecture and AI model governance

success_criteria: Platform processes 50,000+ customer interactions daily with <200ms response time
AI recommendation engine achieves 25%+ click-through rate on personalized offers
NPS score improvement from +32 to +50 within 12 months
Zero critical security vulnerabilities in penetration testing
99.99% uptime SLA maintained throughout first year

step_2_scope.title: Scope Definition

functional: Real-time customer 360° profile aggregation from 12 source systems
ML-powered next-best-action recommendation engine with A/B testing framework
Natural Language Processing (NLP) chatbot for customer service automation
Predictive analytics dashboard for churn risk and lifetime value scoring
Regulatory compliance engine for GDPR/CCPA data subject requests
Mobile SDK for iOS and Android integration
RESTful API gateway for third-party fintech integrations
technical: Real-time streaming data pipeline using Apache Kafka and Spark
Data lake implementation with Delta Lake for ACID transactions
MLOps pipeline with model versioning and A/B testing
CI/CD automation with GitHub Actions and Terraform
End-to-end encryption at rest and in transit
Disaster recovery with multi-region failover (RPO < 5 minutes, RTO < 30 minutes)
services: Requirements gathering and business process analysis (4 weeks)
UX/UI design with accessibility compliance (WCAG 2.1 AA)
Full-stack development and AI model development
System integration with existing core banking (FIS Profile)
Data migration of 5 years historical customer data
User acceptance testing and performance optimization
Production deployment and hypercare support (90 days)

out_of_scope: Legacy mainframe modernization (staying on FIS Profile)
ATM network integration or physical branch systems
Credit scoring algorithm development (using existing vendor)
Mobile banking app complete rewrite (SDK integration only)
Data center hardware procurement and management
assumptions: Nexus will provide dedicated API access to all 12 source systems within 2 weeks of kickoff
AWS infrastructure accounts and networking will be provisioned by client IT team
Legacy system SMEs will be available for 10 hours/week during integration phase
Client will handle all regulatory filings and legal reviews
Third-party data enrichment provider (Experian) contracts are already in place
DataMind will have VPN access to non-production environments
constraints: Must maintain PCI-DSS Level 1 compliance throughout project
No customer PII can be stored outside US-East and US-West AWS regions
All changes to core banking integration require 2-week change window notice
System must handle 10x traffic spikes during tax season (March-April)
Maximum 4-hour maintenance windows allowed (2 AM - 6 AM EST)
All code must pass security scans (SonarQube) with zero critical issues

step_3_delivery.title: Delivery Model
step_3_delivery.methodology: Agile Scrum with SAFe (Scaled Agile Framework) for enterprise alignment

ceremonies.participants: Product Owner + Tech Lead + Key Developers

artifacts: Product Backlog (prioritized user stories in Jira)
Sprint Backlog (committed stories for current sprint)
Definition of Done checklist (automated tests, code review, security scan)
step_3_delivery.location_model: Hybrid - Development team based in DataMind Austin office with 2 days/week remote flexibility. Client stakeholders participate remotely with monthly on-site alignment days at Nexus NYC headquarters.

step_4_integrations.title: Technical Integrations
step_4_integrations.integration_architecture: Event-driven microservices with API Gateway pattern, using Apache Kafka as the central nervous system for real-time data streaming.

system_integrations.integration_method: REST API with API key auth

customer_master.matching_logic: Deterministic matching on SSN + email + phone; probabilistic ML matching for edge cases
customer_master.refresh_frequency: Real-time for transactional data, nightly for enrichment data

integration_testing: Contract testing with Pact for all API integrations
End-to-end integration tests in staging environment
Chaos engineering tests for failure scenarios
Performance testing at 2x expected peak load

key_activities: Technical architecture design and AWS well-architected review
Data model design and source system analysis
Detailed project plan and resource onboarding

milestones.name: Design Review Complete
milestones.criteria: All designs approved by architecture board and business stakeholders

key_activities: CI/CD pipeline setup with GitHub Actions
Development environment setup and team onboarding

milestones.name: Infrastructure Ready
milestones.criteria: All environments provisioned, security approved, CI/CD operational

key_activities: Integration adapters for FIS and Salesforce

milestones.name: MVP Complete
milestones.criteria: Core data pipeline operational, basic recommendations working in dev

key_activities: Churn prediction model training and validation
NLP chatbot training and intent classification
ML model monitoring and drift detection

milestones.name: Feature Complete
milestones.criteria: All features implemented and passed internal QA

key_activities: User acceptance testing with Nexus team

milestones.name: Production Launch
milestones.criteria: Platform live in production, all critical systems operational

dependencies.dependency: AWS account provisioning and VPN setup

third_party_costs.description: Software licenses and third-party services (passed through at cost)

change_management.process: All scope changes require a formal Change Request (CR) document. CRs will be evaluated within 3 business days with impact assessment on timeline and budget. Changes <$10K approved by Project Owner; $10K-$50K require Executive Sponsor approval; >$50K require contract amendment.
change_management.rate_card: Additional resources billed at standard rates. No markup on third-party costs. Expenses reimbursed at actual cost with receipts.

step_7_quality.title: Quality Assurance
step_7_quality.quality_policy: Zero-defect philosophy with continuous quality improvement. All deliverables must meet or exceed industry standards for financial services software.

testing_levels.coverage_target: All API endpoints and data flows
All APIs under load; 2x peak capacity
testing_levels.tools: k6, Gatling, AWS Distributed Load Testing

test_data_management.synthetic_data: Faker-based synthetic data generation for 100K test customers
test_data_management.data_masking: Production data anonymized using k-anonymity algorithms for UAT
test_data_management.refresh_cycle: Test data refreshed weekly in non-production environments

severity_levels.critical: Production blocker, security vulnerability, data loss risk - Fix within 24 hours
severity_levels.high: Major functionality impacted - Fix within 3 business days
severity_levels.medium: Minor functionality issue - Fix within 1 sprint
severity_levels.low: Cosmetic or enhancement - Prioritized in backlog

defect_management.process: All defects logged in Jira with severity, reproduction steps, and environment details. Daily defect triage meeting during testing phases. Root cause analysis required for Critical and High defects.
defect_management.exit_criteria: Zero Critical defects, <5 High defects (with documented workarounds), 95% of Medium defects resolved

model_validation: A/B testing framework for model comparison

code_quality_gates: SonarQube quality gate passed (0 critical/major issues)
Security scan with zero high/critical vulnerabilities
All unit tests passing with >85% coverage
Code review approved by 2 senior engineers

steering_committee.purpose: Strategic decisions, budget oversight, escalation resolution

project_governance_board.purpose: Progress review, risk assessment, scope decisions

technical_architecture_board.purpose: Technical decisions, architecture reviews, security approvals

risk_register.description: Legacy system integration complexity exceeds estimates
risk_register.mitigation: Early proof-of-concept with FIS Profile; dedicated integration specialist; buffer time in schedule

risk_register.description: ML model accuracy below target thresholds
risk_register.mitigation: Phased model rollout with fallback rules; extensive feature engineering; external ML consultant on standby
Early compliance engagement; pre-submission review; compliance checkpoints each sprint
Cross-training on critical skills; documented runbooks; backup resource pool identified

risk_register.description: Data quality issues in source systems
risk_register.mitigation: Data profiling in discovery phase; data quality SLAs with source system owners; cleansing pipeline
Strict change control process; MVP prioritization framework; weekly backlog grooming
Circuit breaker patterns; graceful degradation; multi-region failover for critical paths

escalation_matrix.issue_type: Technical or process issues within team

documentation_requirements: Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) for all significant technical decisions
Change logs for all production deployments
Access control and privilege review logs
Data processing activity records for GDPR

contract_details.contract_type: Master Service Agreement (MSA) with Statement of Work

liability.limitation: Each party's liability limited to fees paid under this SOW ($2.45M) except for: (a) indemnification obligations, (b) confidentiality breaches, (c) IP infringement, (d) fraud/gross negligence
liability.exclusions: No limit on data breach liabilities affecting >10,000 customers

datamind_indemnifies: Breach of data security by DataMind personnel
Negligence or willful misconduct of DataMind team
nexus_indemnifies: Use of deliverables in violation of agreement
Breach of applicable regulations by Nexus

intellectual_property.work_product: All custom code, documentation, and deliverables created specifically for this project - Nexus receives full ownership upon final payment
intellectual_property.pre_existing_ip: DataMind retains ownership of pre-existing tools, frameworks, and utilities; Nexus receives perpetual license
intellectual_property.open_source: All open source components documented with license compliance; no GPL/AGPL in production code without written approval
intellectual_property.ml_models: Trained model weights and inference code - Nexus ownership; base algorithms - DataMind license
intellectual_property.moral_rights: Full waiver of moral rights in all jurisdictions

confidentiality.definition: All non-public information disclosed during project
confidentiality.obligation: Both parties maintain strict confidentiality for 5 years post-termination
confidentiality.data_handling: All customer data encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.3); data deletion within 30 days of contract end

for_convenience.payment: All work completed and accepted through termination date; pro-rata for in-progress milestones

for_cause.payment: No additional payments; return of all materials

post_termination: Delivery of all source code and documentation

warranty.defect_correction: DataMind corrects defects at no additional cost
warranty.disclaimer: No warranty that system will be error-free in all conditions

insurance.certificate: Certificate of Insurance provided within 5 days of SOW execution

data_protection.gdpr_dpa: Data Processing Addendum executed for EU data subjects
data_protection.subprocessors: List of approved subprocessors (AWS, Twilio, DataDog); 30-day notice for new subprocessors
data_protection.breach_notification: 24 hours for any suspected data breach
data_protection.audit_rights: Nexus right to audit DataMind security controls annually

business_alignment.item: Project vision aligns with strategic objectives
SMART objectives are measurable and achievable

delivery_plan.item: Timeline is realistic with appropriate buffers

checks_passed.details: All in-scope/out-of-scope items validated against business requirements
Resource allocation supports timeline; no obvious conflicts
AWS services and tools verified as compatible
Testing coverage aligns with quality requirements
Ownership and licensing terms are clear

warnings.warning: Integration complexity with FIS Profile is rated High
warnings.recommendation: Consider 2-week POC before full development to validate assumptions

warnings.warning: ML model accuracy targets are aggressive
warnings.recommendation: Define fallback rules and phased rollout plan

approvers.name: Michael Rodriguez
approvers.comments: Requirements are well-defined and aligned with business needs.
